summary: The test suite covers the four core functional areas (status classification, report grouping, markdown formatting, JSON formatting) and will catch obvious regressions in the happy path. However, several meaningful gaps reduce confidence: (1) No boundary-condition tests — expiration exactly on the reference date, or daysUntilExpiration exactly equal to the warning window, are never exercised, so the < vs <= boundary in Get-SecretStatus is untested. (2) The configurable warning window is hardcoded to 7 in every test; varying it to confirm behavior changes is never attempted. (3) No empty-input test — an empty SecretConfigs array is not exercised. (4) No error-handling tests despite the requirement for 'meaningful error messages' (malformed date, missing keys, invalid policy values). (5) The first 'Parsing secret configuration' test is trivially asserting on a plain hashtable literal with no implementation code exercised, adding no real value. (6) The JSON test uses ConvertFrom-Json on the output and only checks one bucket; the markdown test does not verify table row content for non-ok secrets. Design is adequate — Context/BeforeEach structure is sensible, test names are descriptive, and dot-sourcing in BeforeAll is the correct Pester pattern — but inline fixtures are repeated across tests rather than centralised in a BeforeAll fixture set.
